title: crepidomanes parvulum
intro: Crepidomanes parvulum, also known as the dwarf spleenwort, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Crepidomanes parvulum include reducing fever and inflammation, as well as treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparation of Crepidomanes parvulum typically involves drying the plant material and then using it in teas, infusions, or tinctures.
